paper.title: Cationic cholesterol-dependent LNP delivery to lung stem cells, the liver, and heart
paper.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

formulations.targeting_details: Cationic cholesterol analogs as structural component. Different from cationic helper lipid approach (DOTAP). Charge on cholesterol vs charge on helper lipid leads to different tropism profiles.
formulations.formulation_notes: 196 LNPs tested. Cationic cholesterol analogs shift tropism differently than cationic helper lipids. Combining cationic cholesterol + cationic helper lipid enables heart and lung stem cell delivery.

results.liver_metric: Normalized delivery across 22 cell types in 5 organs
results.liver_notes: Key finding: cationic cholesterol yields DIFFERENT lung:liver ratios than cationic helper lipid (DOTAP). Combining both enables heart delivery + lung stem cell delivery.

experiments.figure_source: Multiple figures (PNAS open access via PMC)
experiments.notes: 196 LNPs, 22 cell types, 5 organs (liver, lung, spleen, heart, + 1 more). Barcoded screen + Cre/tdTom validation. scRNA-seq used for cell-type resolution. Extends SORT concept: which component carries the charge matters.

key_findings_for_project: Which component carries the charge matters: cationic cholesterol ≠ cationic helper lipid for tropism
196 LNPs, 22 cell types across 5 organs - large barcoded dataset
Combining cationic cholesterol + cationic helper lipid enables HEART + lung stem cell delivery
Extends the SORT paradigm: it's not just 'cationic = lung' but 'which lipid component is cationic' matters
BM cell types NOT measured (only liver, lung, spleen, heart)
Lung stem cell delivery is novel but not directly relevant to HSC project
Dahlman lab - compatible methodology with Kim screen data

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Different approach entirely. Radmand explores charge on cholesterol; Breda uses antibody targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Same lab (Dahlman), compatible barcoding methodology. Extends the design space that Kim's BM-targeting screen explored. The cholesterol charge concept could potentially be applied to BM-targeting formulations.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No BM data. Cannot place on Pareto.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Expands understanding of how charge distribution across LNP components affects tropism. Useful for rational design but no direct HSC relevance.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Medium. The 196 LNP compositions could add to the training set if per-formulation data is extracted from the full paper. However, readout is lung/liver/spleen/heart, not BM.

limitations_and_caveats: BM not measured as a separate organ
Barcoded screen data behind PNAS paywall (though PMC may have full text)
Per-formulation compositions need extraction from supplementary tables
Heart delivery is novel finding but not relevant to HSC project
Lung stem cells are different from BM HSCs - different niche biology
